rationale: Full re-review after PR #40 amended the DI text (strict exit-code discrimination) and added test_mutation_probe_only_a_genuine_test_failure_is_a_kill. All probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/story_audit/mutation.py (the module under review IS the probe machinery: the running process keeps the imported original while the pytest subprocess re-imports the mutant). Core clauses (test_mutation_probe_applies_reports_and_restores): (1) applies the mutation live: writing the original instead of original.replace(find,replace,1) KILLED (stub runner asserts 'VALUE = 2' on disk during the run); (2) reports killed: inverting the mapping '"killed": outcome == TESTS_FAILED' -> '(outcome != TESTS_FAILED)' KILLED; (3) reports survived: '"survived": outcome == TESTS_PASSED' -> '(outcome != TESTS_PASSED)' KILLED; (4) always restores: the finally-block restore write -> pass KILLED (file==original asserted after killed AND survived paths, plus res['restored']); (5) exactly-once application (ambiguous site rejected): occurrences != 1 -> occurrences < 1 KILLED ('error' in result + file untouched). Exit-code discrimination (test_mutation_probe_only_a_genuine_test_failure_is_a_kill): (6) only a genuine test failure is a kill: 'if proc.returncode == 1:' -> 'if proc.returncode:' (the old any-nonzero-is-FAILED behavior) KILLED (exit-5 no-tests-collected must come back as an error, not TESTS_FAILED); (7) an unclean run is reported as an error, never a kill: dropping the 'if outcome not in (TESTS_PASSED, TESTS_FAILED):' guard ('if False:') KILLED (an error outcome would silently become a killed=False/survived=False dict with no 'error' key); (8) no-tests-collected is identified as such: exit-5 message condition '== 5' -> '== 55' KILLED ('no tests matched' asserted in the outcome). Defense clauses (test_mutation_probe_restore_survives_interruption): (9) journal-before-mutating: journaling '' instead of the original KILLED (runner reads the journal mid-window), and journal left behind after a normal probe (unlink -> exists) KILLED; (10) interrupted-probe recovery: neutering the restore write in recover_interrupted_probe KILLED (res['recovered'], file==original, journal gone, recover_interrupted_probe(src) is False afterwards); (11) SIGTERM during the probe window converted to an exception: handler body 'raise KeyboardInterrupt(...)' -> pass KILLED (pytest.raises(KeyboardInterrupt) + file restored + no journal). The handler-NOT-installed mutant (signal.signal -> signal.getsignal), recorded KILLED by the prior review under the old any-nonzero logic, now correctly replays as ERROR (exit -15: the raised TERM kills the inner pytest itself) and is recorded below as documentation, not a claim -- the clause is pinned by the neutered-handler kill instead; (12) unique-ns mtime observable: constant mtime os.utime ns=(1,1) KILLED and second-truncated mtime (int(time.time())*1e9) KILLED via len({before,during,after})==3. UNCOVERED CLAUSE (hence partial): 'every write invalidates the bytecode cache so a same-second size-preserving mutation cannot run stale' is NOT verified by the tests -- and is empirically FALSE on CPython 3.11: the pyc validation key truncates mtime to whole seconds ((mtime-seconds, size)), so distinct nanosecond mtimes within the same second do not invalidate the cache. Executed evidence: the size-PRESERVING survived-inversion mutant ('"survived": outcome == TESTS_PASSED' -> '== TESTS_FAILED', both replacement tokens 12 chars) SURVIVED 2 of 3 runs because the inner pytest was served stale bytecode of the ORIGINAL module (recorded below with result SURVIVED); a minimal out-of-repo reproduction (import, same-size same-second rewrite + unique-ns os.utime, fresh-interpreter re-import) also served the stale module. The acceptance test asserts three distinct st_mtime_ns values -- an observable that does not entail pyc invalidation; covering the clause needs either a write scheme that changes the (mtime-seconds, size) key or an assertion that a same-second size-preserving mutant is actually re-imported.

probes.find: what = ("no tests matched the selector" if proc.returncode == 5
probes.replace: what = ("no tests matched the selector" if proc.returncode == 55
pass  # SIGTERM ignored by the handler

probes.result: ERROR (inner pytest terminated by the uncaught SIGTERM, exit -15 -- documentation, not a kill claim)
SURVIVED (2 of 3 runs: size-preserving mutant served stale pyc of the original -- the uncovered-clause evidence, not a kill claim)

uncovered_clauses: every write invalidates the bytecode cache so a same-second size-preserving mutation cannot run stale (pyc key is (mtime-seconds, size) on CPython 3.11, and unique-ns bumps within one second do not invalidate -- size-preserving mutant demonstrably ran stale)
